title: memory_read with tier=None silently excludes L1 despite docstring saying 'None for all'
what_failed: Function signature and docstring promised that tier=None searches every tier. The code only searched L3+L2 in that case; the L1 branch is gated on `if tier == 'l1'` (exclusive). Pi reasons about its own capabilities from contracts (docstrings), so contract drift caused wrong self-descriptions in chat.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py memory_read() — `if tier == 'l1':` (line 96) excluded L1 from the implicit search; docstring said 'l1/l2/l3 or None for all'.
why_likely: L1 (raw_wiki) may be large; an early author probably wanted to avoid expensive scans on broad queries and quietly excluded it without updating the docstring.
reproduction: Phase 0 audit confirmed via code reading. Phase 3 added testing/test_memory_tier_contract.py: 3 tests that assert (1) docstring no longer claims 'None for all', (2) docstring explicitly marks L1 as opt-in, (3) code behaviour unchanged (the `tier == 'l1'` gate stays). Pre-fix the first two tests fail, the third passes. Post-fix all three pass.
expected: Either include L1 in the implicit search, or update docstring so reality matches contract.
actual: Pre-fix: docstring lied. Post-fix: docstring corrected to 'tier=None searches L3+L2 only; L1 is opt-in via tier=l1 explicitly'. Code-behaviour change deferred to a future phase when raw_wiki has a proper full-text index.
suggested_fix: Conservative: docstring correction only (chosen). Aggressive: `if tier in ('l1', None):` with low default limit (deferred — requires faster L1 query layer).

tests_passing: docstring no longer claims 'None for all'
docstring explicitly marks L1 as opt-in
verification.regression_check: testing/test_memory.py — 5/5 pre-existing tests still pass post-fix (no behaviour regression on L3 or L2 paths).

scope_disclaimer: T-017 closes the docstring-vs-code drift only. The aggressive fix (include L1 in tier=None searches) is deferred. T-017 closure does not imply that L1 search itself is well-tested or fast.
